INITIAL CODES
High Parental Expectations
Conditional Positive Regard
Fear of Failure 
Social Withdrawal
Test Anxiety

High Parental Expectations, Conditional Positive Regard (External stressor and Psychological Origin)
Manifestations of Bad coping habits and Anxiety (Fear of failure, Test Anxiety, Social Withdrawal)

Initial Codes

Academic Anxiety
Parental Pressure
Having self-worth tied to Grades
Unhealthy coping mechanisms
The need for emotional support
Themes

Pressure and Isolation as a result

Academic stress affects self perception

 

In reading then analyzing the transcript given, some recurring ideas  emerged such as the participant's anxiety before exam, fear of disappointing their parents, and feelings of exhaustion. This gives me the idea for the theme of pressure and isolation as a result. It shows that the participant's acknowledgement that they need or want support and that they acknowledge that they experience isolation too. Academic stress affects self perception is another theme for it shows in the transcription that the participant's self image is based or dominated by their academic performance. These 2 themes put point on how external expectations and their own internalized performance standards affects their wellbeing. 

 

Now for the challenge that I have experience in the theme and coding development would be distinguishing the two emotions, anxiety and guilt, from one another. Those two usually appeared together and they also influence one another. Knowing what shows anxiety and what shows guilt form the participant's answer will help me properly and correctly provide the right themes and codes.

messages.content: # FOLLOW THIS WRITING STYLE: • SHOULD use clear, simple language. • SHOULD be spartan and informative. • SHOULD use short, impactful sentences. • SHOULD use active voice; avoid passive voice. • SHOULD focus on practical, actionable insights. • SHOULD use bullet point lists in social media posts. • SHOULD use data and examples to support claims when possible. • SHOULD use “you” and “your” to directly address the reader. • AVOID using em dashes (—) anywhere in your response. Use only commas, periods, or other standard punctuation. If you need to connect ideas, use a period or a semicolon, but never an em dash. • AVOID constructions like "...not just this, but also this". • AVOID metaphors and clichés. • AVOID generalizations. • AVOID common setup language in any sentence, including: in conclusion, in closing, etc. • AVOID output warnings or notes, just the output requested. • AVOID unnecessary adjectives and adverbs. • AVOID hashtags. • AVOID semicolons. • AVOID markdown. • AVOID asterisks. • AVOID these words: “can, may, just, that, very, really, literally, actually, certainly, probably, basically, could, maybe, delve, embark, enlightening, esteemed, shed light, craft, crafting, imagine, realm, game-changer, unlock, discover, skyrocket, abyss, not alone, in a world where, revolutionize, disruptive, utilize, utilizing, dive deep, tapestry, illuminate, unveil, pivotal, intricate, elucidate, hence, furthermore, realm, however, harness, exciting, groundbreaking, cutting-edge, remarkable, it, remains to be seen, glimpse into, navigating, landscape, stark, testament, in summary, in conclusion, moreover, boost, skyrocketing, opened up, powerful, inquiries, ever-evolving" # IMPORTANT: Review your response and ensure no em dashes!

Instructions: 

1.    Read on evidence-based treatment/ interventions for mood disorders from published journal articles.

2.    Answer the questions below.

3.    Get your answers from latest journal articles and NOT just from any article or website available in the internet. 

4.    See APA style for correct citations and referencing.

5.   See rubric.

6. Do not send me a link to your google drive to access your answer.

Questions:

1.    What are some evidence-based treatment/ interventions proven to be effective in treatment of various mood disorders? Describe each and indicate how effective they are.

2.    Will these evidence-based treatment/ interventions work effectively with the Filipino clients/ patients who suffer from various mood disorders? 

A.    If yes, in what ways will they benefit from these evidence-based treatments?

B.    If not, what alternative treatment/ interventions will you propose then? How will these work with the Filipino clients/ patients?

3.    Your references:



RUBRIC

10

Very Good

Answers are accurate and complete. Key points are

stated and supported. Adequate use of appropriate concepts.

10

Very Good

Answer/ ideas shared a deep and thoughtful reflection.

Very Good

Cited sources both in the text itself and the references page, and the citations and references page entries do not have errors in format (APA style)





Formulate an essay with in text citations from credible sources with APA style 7th gen citation reference

7:30 AM - Leave Imus (Aguinaldo Hwy ~1.5hr)

9:30-11:00 AM - Patio Espresso Café (coffee + crepes she wanted;  lake views)

11:30 AM-1:00 PM - La Creperie Twin Lakes (more desserts, volcano photo spot;  10min walk/drive)

1:30-3:00 PM - Crosswinds Garden Walk (free windmills stroll, photo ops; European vibe)

3:30-5:30 PM - French Bakery Quest+ (pastries + coffee break;  chill terrace)

6:00-8:00 PM - Dinner Bag of Beans Main (pasta + cheesecake; garden lights)

8:30-11:00 PM - Drive home (~1hr light traffic)

Hi po Tito/Tita! Good evening po.

Gusto ko lang po sana ipaalam si Princess sa valentines day po. Mag cafe hopping po sana kami this valentines, share ko na din po yung buong plan ko for that day:

Alis po kami by 8, mga 9:30 nasa Patio Espresso na po kami sa Tagaytay niyan for breakfast. Then punta po kami La Creperie Twin Lakes for desserts mga 11:30 AM. After nun, lakad-lakad po kami sa Crosswinds Garden Walk mga 1:30 PM (yung may windmills po). Tapos merienda po sa French Bakery mga 3:30 PM, then dinner na po sa Bag of Beans around 6 PM.

Uwi na po kami after dinner, mga 11 PM nasa bahay na po kami.

I'll keep you updated po throughout the day and mag-iingat po ako. Sana po okay lang sa inyo!

Matcha (greens)
